content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all prime numbers in the range [LLL, RRR].


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space separated integers, LLL and RRR - The start and end of the range.


Output Format

Print on a single line, all prime numbers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) in increasing order with a space between them.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of input contains two space-separated integers, L and R, denoting the start and end of the range.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print all prime numbers in the inclusive range [L, R] in increasing order, separated by a single space. If there is no prime in the range, print an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The primes between 2 and 11 inclusive are 2, 3, 5, 7 and 11.
Only one number (11) is in the range and it is prime, so it is printed.
The range contains only the number 1, which is not prime, therefore an empty line is printed.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm, which is an efficient method for finding all primes smaller than a given number. We create a boolean array, isPrime, of size 1001, where isPrime[i] represents whether i is a prime or not. We initialize all values as true, assuming all numbers are prime initially. Then, we start from 2 and mark all its multiples as non-prime. We repeat this process for all numbers up to the square root of 1000. Finally, for each test case, we iterate over the range [L, R] and print all numbers that are marked as prime.
solution.timeComplexity: O((R - L + 1) + 1000 log log 1000) total. The precomputation of the Sieve of Eratosthenes takes O(1000 log log 1000) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1001) — the dominant structure is the boolean array isPrime.

code.Python3:
import sys
MAX_N=1000
is_prime=[True]*(MAX_N+1)
is_prime[0]=is_prime[1]=False
for p in range(2,int(MAX_N**0.5)+1):
    if is_prime[p]:
        for m in range(p*p,MAX_N+1,p):
            is_prime[m]=False
L,R=map(int,sys.stdin.readline().split())
primes=[str(i) for i in range(L,R+1) if is_prime[i]]
print(' '.join(primes))
